subject: Databases
title: The Full Table Scan
description: A fast breakdown of The Full Table Scan for builders.

hook.text: A query searched 50 million rows to find one customer. It took 12 seconds. That's a full table scan.

buildup.text: Without an index, the database reads every single row to check if it matches the WHERE clause.

discovery.text: Full scans read the entire table from disk. For large tables, that's gigabytes of data to sift through.

twist.text: Sometimes a full scan is correct. If you need 90% of the rows, an index actually makes it slower.

climax.text: The goal: make selective queries use indexes and let analytical scans read sequentially.

punchline.text: Full scans aren't always bad. But for selective queries, they're deadly.

quiz.question: When is a full table scan actually more efficient than using an index?
options: When the query needs to read the majority of rows in the table
When the table has a primary key
Full table scans are never efficient
